question: He said, 'Alas! I am undone.'
options: He exclaimed with sorrow that he was undone.
He said sadly that he is undone.
He exclaimed with joy that he was undone.
He told that alas he was undone.
answer: He exclaimed with sorrow that he was undone.
explanation: The word 'Alas' expresses sorrow, so we use 'exclaimed with sorrow/grief'. ✅

question: The boy said, 'Hurrah! We have won the match.'
options: The boy exclaimed with joy that they had won the match.
The boy said with joy that they have won the match.
The boy exclaimed that we had won the match.
The boy shouted that they won the match.
answer: The boy exclaimed with joy that they had won the match.
explanation: 'Hurrah' expresses joy. Present Perfect 'have won' changes to Past Perfect 'had won'. ✅

question: She said, 'May God bless you!'
options: She prayed that God might bless me.
She wished that God may bless me.
She said that God might bless me.
She blessed me that God might bless you.
answer: She prayed that God might bless me.
explanation: For prayers/wishes involving God, 'prayed' is the correct reporting verb. 'May' changes to 'might'. ✅

question: He said, 'What a beautiful flower it is!'
options: He exclaimed with surprise that it was a very beautiful flower.
He said that what a beautiful flower it was.
He exclaimed that it is a very beautiful flower.
He told that the flower was beautiful.
answer: He exclaimed with surprise that it was a very beautiful flower.
explanation: Exclamatory sentences starting with 'What/How' are changed into assertive sentences using 'very' or 'great'. ✅

question: The old man said, 'Would that I were young again!'
options: The old man wished that he had been young again.
The old man wished that he were young again.
The old man said that he was young again.
The old man strongly wished to be young again.
answer: The old man wished that he had been young again.
explanation: 'Would that' expresses a strong wish. In indirect speech, 'were' changes to 'had been' to reflect the past hypothetical nature. ✅

question: They said, 'Bravo! You have done well.'
options: They applauded him, saying that he had done well.
They exclaimed with joy that he had done well.
They said bravo that he had done well.
They told him that he did well.
answer: They applauded him, saying that he had done well.
explanation: 'Bravo' is best reported using 'applauded + object + saying that'. ✅

question: She said to me, 'Happy Christmas!'
options: She wished me a Happy Christmas.
She said to me Happy Christmas.
She told me that it was a Happy Christmas.
She wished that I had a Happy Christmas.
answer: She wished me a Happy Christmas.
explanation: Greetings like 'Happy Birthday/Christmas' are reported using 'wished'. ✅

question: He said, 'Goodbye, my friends!'
options: He bade goodbye to his friends.
He said goodbye to his friends.
He wished goodbye to his friends.
He told his friends goodbye.
answer: He bade goodbye to his friends.
explanation: For farewells like 'Goodbye' or 'Goodnight', the verb 'bade' (past of bid) is traditionally used. ✅

question: The captain said, 'How terrible the storm is!'
options: The captain exclaimed that the storm was very terrible.
The captain said that how terrible the storm was.
The captain exclaimed with sorrow that the storm is terrible.
The captain told that the storm was terrible.
answer: The captain exclaimed that the storm was very terrible.
explanation: 'How' becomes 'very' in the assertive form. ✅

question: I said to him, 'May you live long!'
options: I wished that he might live long.
I prayed that he might live long.
I blessed him that he might live long.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: Depending on the relation, 'wished' or 'blessed' can be used for 'May you live long'. ✅

question: She said, 'Oh! I lost my purse.'
options: She exclaimed with regret that she had lost her purse.
She said that she lost her purse.
She exclaimed that she lost her purse.
She told sadly that she has lost her purse.
answer: She exclaimed with regret that she had lost her purse.
explanation: 'Oh' can express regret or sorrow. Simple Past 'lost' changes to Past Perfect 'had lost'. ✅

question: He said, 'Fie! A soldier and afraid of death!'
options: He exclaimed with contempt that a soldier should be afraid of death.
He said that it was a shame for a soldier to be afraid of death.
He rebuked the soldier for being afraid of death.
All the above are correct.
answer: All the above are correct.
explanation: 'Fie' expresses contempt or shame. The sentence can be reported by capturing that emotion. ✅

question: They said, 'Long live the King!'
options: They prayed that the King might live long.
They wished that the King lived long.
They shouted that the King live long.
They told that the King might live long.
answer: They prayed that the King might live long.
explanation: This is an optative sentence where 'may' is hidden (May the King live long). ✅

question: He said, 'How fast you run!'
options: He exclaimed that I ran very fast.
He said that I ran very fast.
He exclaimed that how fast I ran.
He told me that I was running fast.
answer: He exclaimed that I ran very fast.
explanation: 'How' becomes 'very' and simple present 'run' becomes 'ran'. ✅

question: She said, 'Pooh! What a dirty child!'
options: She exclaimed with disgust that he was a very dirty child.
She said that he was a dirty child.
She exclaimed that the child is dirty.
She told that pooh the child was dirty.
answer: She exclaimed with disgust that he was a very dirty child.
explanation: 'Pooh' expresses disgust. ✅

question: My friend said to me, 'Good morning!'
options: My friend wished me a good morning.
My friend told me good morning.
My friend said good morning to me.
My friend bade me a good morning.
answer: My friend wished me a good morning.
explanation: 'Good morning' is a wish. ✅

question: He said, 'What a fool I am!'
options: He exclaimed with regret that he was a great fool.
He said that he was a fool.
He confessed that he was a great fool.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: When 'What' is followed by a noun (fool), it changes to 'great'. ✅

question: The audience said, 'What a fine hit!'
options: The audience exclaimed with joy that it was a very fine hit.
The audience said that it was a fine hit.
The audience cheered that it was a fine hit.
The audience shouted what a fine hit it was.
answer: The audience exclaimed with joy that it was a very fine hit.
explanation: Assertive conversion: It was a very fine hit. ✅

question: She said, 'Had I the wings of a dove!'
options: She wished that she had the wings of a dove.
She wished that she had had the wings of a dove.
She said that she has the wings of a dove.
She exclaimed that she had the wings of a dove.
answer: She wished that she had had the wings of a dove.
explanation: Strong wish with 'Had I...' changes to Past Perfect 'she had had'. ✅

question: The teacher said to the student, 'May you succeed!'
options: The teacher wished that the student might succeed.
The teacher told the student that he might succeed.
The teacher said that the student might succeed.
The teacher prayed for the student's success.
answer: The teacher wished that the student might succeed.
explanation: A wish for success. ✅

question: He said, 'Ugh! There is a fly in my soup.'
options: He exclaimed with disgust that there was a fly in his soup.
He said with disgust that there is a fly in his soup.
He exclaimed that a fly was in his soup.
He told that ugh a fly was in his soup.
answer: He exclaimed with disgust that there was a fly in his soup.
explanation: 'Ugh' expresses disgust. ✅

question: They said, 'How cleverly he has planned the robbery!'
options: They exclaimed with surprise that he had planned the robbery very cleverly.
They said that he had planned the robbery cleverly.
They exclaimed that how cleverly he had planned it.
They told that he has planned the robbery very cleverly.
answer: They exclaimed with surprise that he had planned the robbery very cleverly.
explanation: Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect + 'very' for 'how'. ✅

question: The monk said, 'May peace prevail on earth!'
options: The monk prayed that peace might prevail on earth.
The monk wished that peace prevails on earth.
The monk said that peace might prevail on earth.
The monk prayed for peace on earth.
answer: The monk prayed that peace might prevail on earth.
explanation: A formal prayer. ✅

question: He said, 'By God! I did not steal the watch.'
options: He swore by God that he had not stolen the watch.
He said by God that he did not steal the watch.
He exclaimed with an oath that he had not stolen the watch.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: Oaths are reported using 'swore' or 'exclaimed with an oath'. ✅

question: She said, 'What a pity you couldn't come!'
options: She exclaimed with sorrow that it was a great pity I couldn't come.
She said that it was a pity I couldn't come.
She exclaimed that it is a pity I couldn't come.
She told that she was sorry I couldn't come.
answer: She exclaimed with sorrow that it was a great pity I couldn't come.
explanation: 'What a pity' ➔ 'It was a great pity'. ✅

question: The beggar said, 'May you all go to hell!'
options: The beggar cursed them that they might all go to hell.
The beggar wished that they might all go to hell.
The beggar said that they all go to hell.
The beggar prayed that they might go to hell.
answer: The beggar cursed them that they might all go to hell.
explanation: When a wish is harmful or negative, use 'cursed'. ✅

question: The boy said, 'Alas! My father is dead.'
options: The boy exclaimed with sorrow that his father was dead.
The boy said that his father was dead.
The boy told with grief that his father was dead.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: Reporting a tragic exclamation. ✅

question: She said, 'Goodnight, Mother!'
options: She bade her mother goodnight.
She wished her mother goodnight.
She told her mother goodnight.
She said goodnight to her mother.
answer: She bade her mother goodnight.
explanation: 'Goodnight' is a farewell, so 'bade' is used. ✅

question: He said, 'How happy I am!'
options: He exclaimed with joy that he was very happy.
He said that he was very happy.
He exclaimed that how happy he was.
He told that he is very happy.
answer: He exclaimed with joy that he was very happy.
explanation: Is ➔ Was + 'very'. ✅

question: They said, 'If only we had won!'
options: They wished that they had won.
They wished that they had had won.
They exclaimed with regret that they had not won.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: 'If only' expresses a wish or regret about a past result. ✅
